assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services\");\n26\t    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(2).is_some());\n27\t    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_none());\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\t/// Scenario: Exit gracefully by finding closest parent\n104\t    stack.exit_to_scope(3);\n105\t\n106\t    // Current behavior: finds closest parent (level2 at indent 2)\n107\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), Scenario: Verify that scopes with many keys are properly cleaned up on exit\n427\t/// Expected: All keys should be cleared and no references remain to the exited scope\n428\t#[test]\n429\tfn   let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n554\t\n555\t    // Create deep nesting\n556\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"a\".to_string()));\n557\t    stack.add_key(\"a_key\", 2).unwrap();\n558\t\n559\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 3, Some(\"b\".to_string()));\n560\t    stack.add_key(\"b_key\", 4).unwrap();\n561\t\n562\t    stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"c\".to_string()));\n563\t assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"d_key\"));\n583\t}\n584\t\n585\t/// Scenario: Verify that re-entering a scope after exit gets fresh state\n586\t/// Expected: Re-entering should not inherit state from State Preservation Tests for Scope Exit Operations\n2\t//!\n3\t//! These tests verify that scope state is properly preserved during partial\n4\t//! and complete scope exit operations, ensuring that parent scopes maintain\n5\t//! their keys, metadata, and configuration after child scopes are removed.\n6\t\n7\tuse armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n8\t\n9\t/// Parent Scope Exit Tests\n10\t///\n11\t/// These tests verify that when exiting from a child scope to its parent scope,\n12\t/// the parent scope's state (keys, metadata, flags) is preserved correctly.\n13\t\n14\tmod parent_scope_exit_tests {\n15\t    use super::*;\n16\t\n17\t    /// Test that parent scope keys are preserved after exiting child scope\n18\t    #[test]\n19\t    fn test_parent_scope_keys_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n20\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n21\t\n22\t        // Create parent scope with keys\n23\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n24\t        stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n25\t        stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n26\t\n27\t        // Create child scope with its own keys\n28\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n29\t        stack.add_key(\"host\", 5).unwrap();\n30\t        stack.add_key(\"port\", 6).unwrap();\n31\t\n32\t        // Exit to parent scope\n33\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n34\t\n35\t        // Verify parent scope still has its keys\n36\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"web\"), \"Parent key 'web' should be preserved\");\n37\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"database\"), \"Parent key 'database' should be preserved\");\n38\t\n39\t        // Verify child keys are removed\n40\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"host\"), \"Child key 'host' should be removed\");\n41\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"port\"), \"Child key 'port' should be removed\");\n42\t\n43\t        // Verify we're at the correct scope\n44\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n45\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services\");\n46\t    }\n47\t\n48\t    /// Test that parent scope metadata is preserved after child exit\n49\t    #[test]\n50\t    fn test_parent_scope_metadata_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n51\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n52\t\n53\t        // Create parent scope and set metadata\n54\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n55\t        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = true;\n56\t\n57\t        // Create child scope\n58\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n59\t        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = false; // Different from parent\n60\t\n61\t        // Exit to parent scope\n62\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n63\t\n64\t        // Verify parent metadata is preserved\n65\t        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().is_flow_style,\n66\t                \"Parent flow_style flag should be preserved\");\n67\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().parent_key, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n68\t    }\n69\t\n70\t    /// Test that parent scope line numbers are preserved\n71\t    #[test]\n72\t    fn test_parent_scope_start_line_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n73\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n74\t\n75\t        // Create parent scope at specific line\n76\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n77\t        let parent_start_line = stack.current_scope_ref().start_line;\n78\t\n79\t        // Create child scope\n80\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 10, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n81\t\n82\t        // Exit to parent scope\n83\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n84\t\n85\t        // Verify parent's start line is preserved\n86\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().start_line, parent_start_line,\n87\t                   \"Parent scope start_line should be preserved\");\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Test parent scope key count accuracy after child exit\n91\t    #[test]\n92\t    fn test_parent_scope_key_count_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n93\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n94\t\n95\t        // Create parent scope with multiple keys\n96\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n97\t        for i in 0..10 {\n98\t            stack.add_key(&format!(\"key_{}\", i), 1 + i).unwrap();\n99\t        }\n100\t        let parent_key_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n101\t\n102\t        // Create child scope with more keys\n103\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 20, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n104\t        for i in 0..15 {\n105\t            stack.add_key(&format!(\"child_key_{}\", i), 20 + i).unwrap();\n106\t        }\n107\t\n108\t        // Exit to parent scope\n109\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n110\t\n111\t        // Verify parent key count is unchanged\n112\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), parent_key_count,\n113\t                   \"Parent scope key count should be preserved\");\n114\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 10);\n115\t    }\n116\t\n117\t    /// Test that parent scope sequence context is preserved\n118\t    #[test]\n119\t    fn test_parent_scope_sequence_context_preserved() {\n120\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n121\t\n122\t        // Create parent sequence scope\n123\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n124\t        let parent_item_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n125\t\n126\t        // Create child mapping scope\n127\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n128\t\n129\t        // Exit to parent sequence scope\n130\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n131\t\n132\t        // Verify parent sequence context is preserved\n133\t        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context,\n134\t                \"Parent in_sequence_context should be preserved\");\n135\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, parent_item_id,\n136\t                   \"Parent sequence_item_id should be preserved\");\n137\t    }\n138\t}\n139\t\n140\t/// Grandparent Scope Exit Tests\n141\t///\n142\t/// These tests verify that when exiting from a deep scope to its grandparent,\n143\t/// both the grandparent scope and intermediate parent scopes maintain their state.\n144\t\n145\tmod grandparent_scope_exit_tests {\n146\t    use super::*;\n147\t\n148\t    /// Test that grandparent scope state is preserved after multi-level exit\n149\t    #[test]\n150\t    fn test_grandparent_scope_keys_preserved_after_multi_level_exit() {\n151\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n152\t\n153\t        // Create grandparent scope\n154\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n155\t        stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n156\t        stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n157\t        let grandparent_path = stack.get_scope_path();\n158\t\n159\t        // Create parent scope\n160\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n161\t        stack.add_key(\"config\", 5).unwrap();\n162\t\n163\t        // Create child scope\n164\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 6, Some(\"settings\".to_string()));\n165\t        stack.add_key(\"debug\", 7).unwrap();\n166\t        stack.add_key(\"verbose\", 8).unwrap();\n167\t\n168\t        // Exit from child (6) to grandparent (2)\n169\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n170\t\n171\t        // Verify grandparent scope is current and has its keys\n172\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n173\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), grandparent_path);\n174\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"web\"), \"Grandparent key 'web' should be preserved\");\n175\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"database\"), \"Grandparent key 'database' should be preserved\");\n176\t\n177\t        // Verify child and intermediate parent keys are removed\n178\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"config\"), \"Parent key 'config' should be removed\");\n179\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"debug\"), \"Child key 'debug' should be removed\");\n180\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"verbose\"), \"Child key 'verbose' should be removed\");\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Test that intermediate parent scope is removed during grandparent exit\n184\t    #[test]\n185\t    fn test_intermediate_parent_scope_removed_on_grandparent_exit() {\n186\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n187\t\n188\t        // Create grandparent scope\n189\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n190\t\n191\t        // Create intermediate parent scope\n192\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n193\t\n194\t        // Create child scope\n195\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n196\t\n197\t        // Verify all scopes are present\n198\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + level1 + level2 + level3\n199\t\n200\t        // Exit from child to grandparent (skipping intermediate parent)\n201\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n202\t\n203\t        // Verify intermediate parent is removed\n204\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2); // root + level1\n205\t        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(4).is_none(),\n206\t                \"Intermediate parent at indent 4 should be removed\");\n207\t        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_none(),\n208\t                \"Child scope at indent 6 should be removed\");\n209\t\n210\t        // Verify grandparent is current\n211\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n212\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n213\t    }\n214\t\n215\t    /// Test grandparent metadata preservation through multiple levels\n216\t    #[test]\n217\t    fn stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n255\t        stack.add_key(\"level2_key\", 6).unwrap();\n256\t\n257\t        // Create child scope\n258\t        stack.enter_scope(8, 7, stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n263\t\n264\t        // Verify great-grandparent state is preserved\n265\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n266\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), removed\");\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Test that grandparent scope key count is accurate after multi-level cleanup\n274\t    #[test]\n275\t    fn stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"grandparent\".to_string()));\n280\t        stack.add_key(\"key1\", 2).unwrap();\n281\t        stack.add_key(\"key2\", 3).unwrap();\n282\t        stack.add_key(\"key3\", state preservation in edge cases:\n304\t/// - Root scope preservation\n305\t/// - Single-level scope exit\n306\t/// - Deeply nested scope exit\n307\t/// - Empty scopes\n308\t\n309\tmod edge_case_tests {\n310\t    use super::*;\n311\t\n312\t    /// Test that root scope state is preserved even in aggressive exits\n313\t    #[test]\n314\t    fn test_root_scope_state_preserved_in_aggressive_exits() {\n315\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n316\t\n317\t        // Create deeply nested structure\n318\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n319\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n320\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n321\t\n322\t        // Add a key at root (before any scope entry, this would be in root scope)\n323\t        let original_root_keys = stack.scopes[0].keys.len();\n324\t        let original_root_indent = stack.scopes[0].indent_level;\n325\t\n326\t        // Perform aggressive exit to root\n327\t        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n328\t\n329\t        // Verify root scope state is preserved\n330\t        assert_eq!(stack.scopes[0].keys.len(), original_root_keys,\n331\t                   \"Root scope keys should be preserved\");\n332\t        assert_eq!(stack.scopes[0].indent_level, original_root_indent,\n333\t                   \"Root scope indent should be preserved\");\n334\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1, \"Should only have root scope\");\n335\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0, \"Should be at root indent\");\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    /// Test single-level scope exit state preservation\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_single_level_scope_exit_preserves_root_state() {\n341\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n342\t\n343\t        // Create single nested level\n344\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n345\t        stack.add_key(\"setting\", 2).unwrap();\n346\t\n347\t        // Verify we're in the nested scope\n348\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n349\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n350\t\n351\t        // Exit to root\n352\t        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n353\t\n354\t        // Verify root scope is preserved and child is removed\n355\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n356\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n357\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"setting\"), \"Child key should be removed\");\n358\t        assert!(stack.scopes[0].indent_level == 0, \"Root indent should be 0\");\n359\t    }\n360\t\n361\t    /// Test deeply nested scope exit with state preservation at each level\n362\t    #[test]\n363\t    fn test_deeply_nested_scope_exit_preserves_correct_level_state() {\n364\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n365\t\n366\t        // Create 5 levels of nesting\n367\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n368\t        stack.add_key(\"l1_key\", 2).unwrap();\n369\t\n370\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 3, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n371\t        stack.add_key(\"l2_key\", 4).unwrap();\n372\t\n373\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n374\t        stack.add_key(\"l3_key\", 6).unwrap();\n375\t\n376\t        stack.enter_scope(8, 7, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n377\t        stack.add_key(\"l4_key\", 8).unwrap();\n378\t\n379\t        stack.enter_scope(10, 9, Some(\"level5\".to_string()));\n380\t        stack.add_key(\"l5_key\", 10).unwrap();\n381\t\n382\t        // Exit to level 3 (partial exit)\n383\t        stack.exit_to_scope(6);\n384\t\n385\t        // Verify level 3 state is preserved\n386\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 6);\n387\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2.level3\");\n388\t        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l1_key\"), \"Level 1 key should be preserved\");\n389\t        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l2_key\"), \"Level 2 key should be preserved\");\n390\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"l3_key\"), \"Level 3 key should be preserved (current scope)\");\n391\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l4_key\"), \"Level 4 key should be removed\");\n392\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l5_key\"), \"Level 5 key should be removed\");\n393\t\n394\t        // Verify correct depth\n395\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + level1 + level2 + level3\n396\t    }\n397\t\n398\t    /// Test scope exit when parent scope has no keys\n399\t    #[test]\n400\t    fn test_scope_exit_with_empty_parent_scope() {\n401\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n402\t\n403\t        // Create parent scope with no keys\n404\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"empty_parent\".to_string()));\n405\t        let parent_key_count = }\n421\t\n422\t    /// Test scope exit when target scope doesn't exist (closest parent used)\n423\t    #[test]\n424\t    fn test_scope_exit_to_nonexistent_target_preserves_closest_parent() {\n425\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n426\t\n427\t        // Create scopes at indents 0, 2, 6 (gap at 4)\n428\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n429\t /// Test that scope exit preserves depth tracking accuracy\n446\t    #[test]\n447\t    fn test_scope_exit_preserves_depth_tracking() {\n448\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n449\t\n450\t        // Build up depth\n451\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"a\".to_string()));\n452\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"b\".to_string()));\n453\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"c\".to_string()));\n454\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + 3 scopes\n455\t\n456\t        // Exit and verify depth decreases correctly\n457\t        stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n458\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3); // root + 2 scopes\n459\t\n460\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n461\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2); // root + 1 scope\n462\t\n463\t        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n464\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1); // root only\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    /// Test scope exit with sequence scope in hierarchy\n468\t    #[test]\n469\t    fn test_scope_exit_with_sequence_scope_in_hierarchy() {\n470\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n471\t\n472\t        // Create parent mapping\n473\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n474\t        stack.add_key(\"item1\", 2).unwrap();\n475\t\n476\t        // Create sequence scope\n477\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n478\t        let seq_item_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n479\t\n480\t        // Create nested mapping in sequence\n481\t        stack.enter_scope(6, 4, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n482\t        stack.add_key(\"nested_key\", 5).unwrap();\n483\t\n484\t        // Exit from nested mapping back to sequence\n485\t        stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n486\t\n487\t        // Verify sequence scope state is preserved\n488\t        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context);\n489\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, seq_item_id);\n490\t        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"nested_key\"), \"Nested key should be removed\");\n491\t\n492\t        // Exit from sequence back to mapping\n493\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n494\t\n495\t        // Verify mapping scope state is preserved\n496\t        assert!(!stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context);\n497\t        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id.is_none());\n498\t        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"item1\"), \"Mapping key should be preserved\");\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    /// Test that flow-style flags are preserved correctly through scope exits\n502\t    #[test]\n503\t    fn test_flow_style_flag_preservation_through_scope_exits() {\n504\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n505\t\n506\t        // Create flow-style parent\n507\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"flow_parent\".to_string()));\n508\t        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = true;\n509\t\n510\t        // Create non-flow-style child\n511\t  web:\n639\t        //     host: localhost\n640\t        //     port: 8080\n641\t        //   database:\n642\t        //     host: db.example.com\n643\t        //     port: 5432\n644\t\n645\t State Preservation Tests for Scope Exit Operations\n//!\n//! These tests verify that scope state is properly preserved during partial\n//! and complete scope exit operations, ensuring that parent scopes maintain\n//! their keys, metadata, and configuration after child scopes are removed.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n\n/// Parent Scope Exit Tests\n///\n/// These tests verify that when exiting from a child scope to its parent scope,\n/// the parent scope's state (keys, metadata, flags) is preserved correctly.\n\nmod parent_scope_exit_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test that parent scope keys are preserved after exiting child scope\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_scope_keys_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent scope with keys\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        // Create child scope with its own keys\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 5).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to parent scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent scope still has its keys\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"web\"), \"Parent key 'web' should be preserved\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"database\"), \"Parent key 'database' should be preserved\");\n\n        // Verify child keys are removed\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"host\"), \"Child key 'host' should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"port\"), \"Child key 'port' should be removed\");\n\n        // Verify we're at the correct scope\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that parent scope metadata is preserved after child exit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_scope_metadata_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent scope and set metadata\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = true;\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = false; // Different from parent\n\n        // Exit to parent scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent metadata is preserved\n        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().is_flow_style,\n                \"Parent flow_style flag should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().parent_key, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that parent scope line numbers are preserved\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_scope_start_line_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent scope at specific line\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        let parent_start_line = stack.current_scope_ref().start_line;\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 10, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n\n        // Exit to parent scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent's start line is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().start_line, parent_start_line,\n                   \"Parent scope start_line should be preserved\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test parent scope key count accuracy after child exit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_scope_key_count_preserved_after_child_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent scope with multiple keys\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n        for i in 0..10 {\n            stack.add_key(&format!(\"key_{}\", i), 1 + i).unwrap();\n        }\n        let parent_key_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n\n        // Create child scope with more keys\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 20, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n        for i in 0..15 {\n            stack.add_key(&format!(\"child_key_{}\", i), 20 + i).unwrap();\n        }\n\n        // Exit to parent scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent key count is unchanged\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), parent_key_count,\n                   \"Parent scope key count should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 10);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that parent scope sequence context is preserved\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_scope_sequence_context_preserved() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent sequence scope\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        let parent_item_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n        // Create child mapping scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n\n        // Exit to parent sequence scope\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent sequence context is preserved\n        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context,\n                \"Parent in_sequence_context should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, parent_item_id,\n                   \"Parent sequence_item_id should be preserved\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Grandparent Scope Exit Tests\n///\n/// These tests verify that when exiting from a deep scope to its grandparent,\n/// both the grandparent scope and intermediate parent scopes maintain their state.\n\nmod grandparent_scope_exit_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test that grandparent scope state is preserved after multi-level exit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_grandparent_scope_keys_preserved_after_multi_level_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create grandparent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n        let grandparent_path = stack.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Create parent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"config\", 5).unwrap();\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 6, Some(\"settings\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"debug\", 7).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"verbose\", 8).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit from child (6) to grandparent (2)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify grandparent scope is current and has its keys\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), grandparent_path);\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"web\"), \"Grandparent key 'web' should be preserved\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"database\"), \"Grandparent key 'database' should be preserved\");\n\n        // Verify child and intermediate parent keys are removed\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"config\"), \"Parent key 'config' should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"debug\"), \"Child key 'debug' should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"verbose\"), \"Child key 'verbose' should be removed\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that intermediate parent scope is removed during grandparent exit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_intermediate_parent_scope_removed_on_grandparent_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create grandparent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n\n        // Create intermediate parent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n\n        // Verify all scopes are present\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + level1 + level2 + level3\n\n        // Exit from child to grandparent (skipping intermediate parent)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify intermediate parent is removed\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2); // root + level1\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(4).is_none(),\n                \"Intermediate parent at indent 4 should be removed\");\n        assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_none(),\n                \"Child scope at indent 6 should be removed\");\n\n        // Verify grandparent is current\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test grandparent metadata preservation through multiple levels\n    #[test]\n    fn test_grandparent_metadata_preserved_through_multiple_levels() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create grandparent with metadata\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"root_config\".to_string()));\n        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = true;\n        let grandparent_metadata = stack.current_scope_ref().is_flow_style;\n\n        // Create parent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"section\".to_string()));\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"subsection\".to_string()));\n\n        // Exit to grandparent\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify grandparent metadata is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().is_flow_style, grandparent_metadata,\n                   \"Grandparent is_flow_style should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().parent_key, Some(\"root_config\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    /// Test multi-level grandparent exit (great-grandparent)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_great_grandparent_scope_preserved_after_deep_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create great-grandparent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"root\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Create grandparent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 3, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"level1_key\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // Create parent scope\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"level2_key\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        // Create child scope\n        stack.enter_scope(8, 7, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"level3_key\", 8).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit from child (8) to great-grandparent (2)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify great-grandparent state is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"root\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"), \"Great-grandparent key should be preserved\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level1_key\"), \"Grandparent key should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level2_key\"), \"Parent key should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level3_key\"), \"Child key should be removed\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that grandparent scope key count is accurate after multi-level cleanup\n    #[test]\n    fn test_grandparent_key_count_accurate_after_multi_level_cleanup() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create grandparent with specific key count\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"grandparent\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key1\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key2\", 3).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key3\", 4).unwrap();\n        let expected_key_count = 3;\n\n        // Create intermediate scopes\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"pkey\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 7, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"ckey\", 8).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to grandparent\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify grandparent key count is accurate\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), expected_key_count,\n                   \"Grandparent key count should be accurate after cleanup\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Edge Case Tests\n///\n/// These tests verify state preservation in edge cases:\n/// - Root scope preservation\n/// - Single-level scope exit\n/// - Deeply nested scope exit\n/// - Empty scopes\n\nmod edge_case_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test that root scope state is preserved even in aggressive exits\n    #[test]\n    fn test_root_scope_state_preserved_in_aggressive_exits() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create deeply nested structure\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n\n        // Add a key at root (before any scope entry, this would be in root scope)\n        let original_root_keys = stack.scopes[0].keys.len();\n        let original_root_indent = stack.scopes[0].indent_level;\n\n        // Perform aggressive exit to root\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n        // Verify root scope state is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.scopes[0].keys.len(), original_root_keys,\n                   \"Root scope keys should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.scopes[0].indent_level, original_root_indent,\n                   \"Root scope indent should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1, \"Should only have root scope\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0, \"Should be at root indent\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test single-level scope exit state preservation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_single_level_scope_exit_preserves_root_state() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create single nested level\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"setting\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Verify we're in the nested scope\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n\n        // Exit to root\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n        // Verify root scope is preserved and child is removed\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"setting\"), \"Child key should be removed\");\n        assert!(stack.scopes[0].indent_level == 0, \"Root indent should be 0\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test deeply nested scope exit with state preservation at each level\n    #[test]\n    fn test_deeply_nested_scope_exit_preserves_correct_level_state() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create 5 levels of nesting\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l1_key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 3, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l2_key\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 5, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l3_key\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(8, 7, Some(\"level4\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l4_key\", 8).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(10, 9, Some(\"level5\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l5_key\", 10).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to level 3 (partial exit)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(6);\n\n        // Verify level 3 state is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 6);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2.level3\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l1_key\"), \"Level 1 key should be preserved\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l2_key\"), \"Level 2 key should be preserved\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"l3_key\"), \"Level 3 key should be preserved (current scope)\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l4_key\"), \"Level 4 key should be removed\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"l5_key\"), \"Level 5 key should be removed\");\n\n        // Verify correct depth\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + level1 + level2 + level3\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope exit when parent scope has no keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_with_empty_parent_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent scope with no keys\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"empty_parent\".to_string()));\n        let parent_key_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n        assert_eq!(parent_key_count, 0);\n\n        // Create child scope with keys\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"child_key\", 3).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to empty parent\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify empty parent state is preserved (still empty)\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0,\n                   \"Empty parent should remain empty\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"child_key\"), \"Child key should be removed\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().parent_key, Some(\"empty_parent\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope exit when target scope doesn't exist (closest parent used)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_to_nonexistent_target_preserves_closest_parent() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create scopes at indents 0, 2, 6 (gap at 4)\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l2_key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level6\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"l6_key\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to indent 4 (which doesn't exist)\n        // Should find closest parent (level2 at indent 2)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n\n        // Verify closest parent (level2) state is preserved\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2, \"Should be at closest parent indent\");\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"l2_key\"), \"Closest parent key should be preserved\");\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"l6_key\"), \"Deeper scope key should be removed\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level2\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that scope exit preserves depth tracking accuracy\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_preserves_depth_tracking() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Build up depth\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"a\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"b\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"c\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + 3 scopes\n\n        // Exit and verify depth decreases correctly\n        stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3); // root + 2 scopes\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2); // root + 1 scope\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1); // root only\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope exit with sequence scope in hierarchy\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_with_sequence_scope_in_hierarchy() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create parent mapping\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"item1\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Create sequence scope\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n        let seq_item_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n        // Create nested mapping in sequence\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 4, Some(\"nested\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"nested_key\", 5).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit from nested mapping back to sequence\n        stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n\n        // Verify sequence scope state is preserved\n        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, seq_item_id);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"nested_key\"), \"Nested key should be removed\");\n\n        // Exit from sequence back to mapping\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify mapping scope state is preserved\n        assert!(!stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context);\n        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id.is_none());\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"item1\"), \"Mapping key should be preserved\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that flow-style flags are preserved correctly through scope exits\n    #[test]\n    fn test_flow_style_flag_preservation_through_scope_exits() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create flow-style parent\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"flow_parent\".to_string()));\n        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = true;\n\n        // Create non-flow-style child\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"block_child\".to_string()));\n        stack.current_scope().is_flow_style = false;\n\n        // Exit to parent\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify parent's flow-style flag is preserved\n        assert!(stack.current_scope_ref().is_flow_style,\n                \"Parent flow_style flag should be preserved\");\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().parent_key, Some(\"flow_parent\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope exit with multiple keys at each level\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_with_multiple_keys_per_level() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Create level 1 with multiple keys\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key1a\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key1b\", 3).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key1c\", 4).unwrap();\n        let level1_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n\n        // Create level 2 with multiple keys\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 5, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key2a\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"key2b\", 7).unwrap();\n        let level2_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n\n        // Exit to level 1\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify level 1 state is preserved\n   /// Test that exiting to same indent is idempotent\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exit_to_current_indent_is_idempotent() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"scope\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        let before_depth = stack.depth();\n        let before_indent = stack.current_indent();\n        let before_key_count = stack.current_scope_ref().key_count();\n\n        // Exit to same indent we're already at\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n        // Verify nothing changed\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), before_depth);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), before_indent);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), before_key_count);\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"key\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope exit preserves scope path accuracy\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_exit_preserves_scope_path_accuracy() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Build deep scope path\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n        stack.enter_scope(8, 4, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n\n        let full_path = stack.get_scope_path();\n 4).unwrap();\n\n        stack.enter_scope(9, 5, Some(\"level9\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key9\", 6).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit to level 6\n        stack.exit_to_scope(6);\n\n Test realistic service configuration scenario\n    #[test]\n    fn test_realistic_service_config_scenario() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Simulate parsing:\n        // services scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        // Enter web scope\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // Exit web, enter database (sibling)\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n\n        // Add database keys\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n\n        // Verify final state\n
