{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c5da43e81ffd4133a1ba8816","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// Classification of indent transition types\n76\t///\n77\t/// This enum represents the three possible types of indent transitions\n78\t/// that can occur during YAML parsing.\n79\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n80\tpub enum IndentTransitionType {\n81\t    /// Indent increased, indicating entry into a deeper scope\n82\t    EnterScope,\n83\t    /// Indent decreased, indicating exit to a parent scope\n84\t    ExitScope,\n85\t    /// No indent change, staying at the same scope level\n86\t    SameLevel,\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\timpl IndentTransitionType {\n90\t    /// Classify an indent transition based on from/to indents\n91\t    pub fn classify(from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize) -> Self {\n92\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n93\t        match to_indent.cmp(&from_indent) {\n94\t            Ordering::Greater => Self::EnterScope,\n95\t            Ordering::Less => Self::ExitScope,\n96\t            Ordering::Equal => Self::SameLevel,\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Check if this is an EnterScope transition\n101\t    pub fn is_enter_scope(&self) -> bool {\n102\t        matches!(self, Self::EnterScope)\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    /// Check if this is an ExitScope transition\n106\t    pub fn is_exit_scope(&self) -> bool {\n107\t        matches!(self, Self::ExitScope)\n108\t    }\n109\t\n110\t    /// Check if this is a SameLevel transition\n111\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n112\t        matches!(self, Self::SameLevel)\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransitionType {\n117\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n118\t        match self {\n119\t            Self::EnterScope => write!(f, \"enter-scope\"),\n120\t            Self::ExitScope => write!(f, \"exit-scope\"),\n121\t            Self::SameLevel => write!(f, \"same-level\"),\n122\t        }\n123\t    }\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t/// State machine for tracking indent level transitions\n127\t///\n128\t/// This struct maintains the current state of the parser's indent transition\n129\t/// tracking, enabling classification of scope operations during parsing.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n131\tpub struct IndentTransitionState {\n132\t    /// The previous indentation level\n133\t    pub from_indent: usize,\n134\t    /// The current indentation level\n135\t    pub to_indent: usize,\n136\t    /// The type of the last transition\n137\t    pub last_transition_type: IndentTransitionType,\n138\t    /// Whether the last transition had a key token\n139\t    pub last_had_key: bool,\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\timpl IndentTransitionState {\n143\t    /// Create a new transition state in initial state\n144\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n145\t        Self {\n146\t            from_indent: 0,\n147\t            to_indent: 0,\n148\t            last_transition_type: IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n149\t            last_had_key: false,\n150\t        }\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Update the state with a new indent transition\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// # Arguments\n156\t    ///\n157\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n158\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n159\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n160\t    pub fn update(&mut self, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool) {\n161\t        self.from_indent = from_indent;\n162\t        self.to_indent = to_indent;\n163\t        self.last_transition_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(from_indent, to_indent);\n164\t        self.last_had_key = has_key;\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the current transition type\n168\t    pub fn current_transition_type(&self) -> IndentTransitionType {\n169\t        self.last_transition_type\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Check if the last transition was an enter-scope\n173\t    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n174\t        self.last_transition_type.is_enter_scope()\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Check if the last transition was an exit-scope\n178\t    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n179\t        self.last_transition_type.is_exit_scope()\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Check if the last transition was same-level\n183\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n184\t        self.last_transition_type.is_same_level()\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Get a description of the current state\n188\t    pub fn describe(&self) -> String {\n189\t        format!(\n190\t            \"IndentTransitionState(from={}, to={}, type={}, had_key={})\",\n191\t            self.from_indent, self.to_indent, self.last_transition_type, self.last_had_key\n192\t        )\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    /// Reset to initial state\n196\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n197\t        *self = Self::new();\n198\t    }\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\timpl Default for IndentTransitionState {\n202\t    fn default() -> Self {\n203\t        Self::new()\n204\t    }\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransitionState {\n208\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n209\t        write!(\n210\t            f,\n211\t            \"IndentTransitionState(from={}, to={}, type={}, had_key={})\",\n212\t            self.from_indent, self.to_indent, self.last_transition_type, self.last_had_key\n213\t        )\n214\t    }\n215\t}\n216\t\n217\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n218\t///\n219\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n220\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n221\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n222\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n223\tpub struct Scope {\n224\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n225\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n226\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n227\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n228\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n229\t    pub start_line: usize,\n230\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n231\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n232\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n233\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n234\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n235\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n236\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n237\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl Scope {\n241\t    /// Create a new scope\n242\t    ///\n243\t    /// # Arguments\n244\t    ///\n245\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n246\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n247\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// # Examples\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// ```\n252\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n253\t    ///\n254\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n255\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n256\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n257\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n258\t    /// ```\n259\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n260\t        Self {\n261\t            indent_level,\n262\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n263\t            start_line,\n264\t            parent_key,\n265\t            is_flow_style: false,\n266\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n267\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n268\t        }\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n272\t    ///\n273\t    /// # Arguments\n274\t    ///\n275\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n276\t    ///\n277\t    /// # Returns\n278\t    ///\n279\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n280\t    ///\n281\t    /// # Examples\n282\t    ///\n283\t    /// ```\n284\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n289\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n290\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n291\t    /// ```\n292\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n293\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n297\t    ///\n298\t    /// # Arguments\n299\t    ///\n300\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n301\t    ///\n302\t    /// # Returns\n303\t    ///\n304\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n305\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n306\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n310\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n311\t        self.keys.len()\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n315\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n316\t        self.keys.clear();\n317\t    }\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n321\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n322\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n323\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n324\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n325\t        }\n326\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n327\t    }\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n331\t///\n332\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n333\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n334\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n335\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n336\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n337\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n338\t    pub scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n339\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n340\t    base_indent: usize,\n341\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n342\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n343\t    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n344\t    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n345\t    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n346\t    last_indent: usize,\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\timpl ScopeStack {\n350\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n351\t    ///\n352\t    /// # Arguments\n353\t    ///\n354\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n355\t    ///\n356\t    /// # Examples\n357\t    ///\n358\t    /// ```\n359\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n362\t    /// ```\n363\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n364\t        Self {\n365\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n366\t            base_indent,\n367\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n368\t            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n369\t            last_indent: 0,\n370\t        }\n371\t    }\n372\t\n373\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n374\t    ///\n375\t    /// # Panics\n376\t    ///\n377\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n378\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n379\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// # Panics\n385\t    ///\n386\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n387\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n388\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// # Arguments\n394\t    ///\n395\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n396\t    ///\n397\t    /// # Returns\n398\t    ///\n399\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n400\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n401\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n405\t    ///\n406\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n407\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n408\t    ///\n409\t    /// # Arguments\n410\t    ///\n411\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n412\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n413\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n414\t    ///\n415\t    /// # Examples\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// ```\n418\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n419\t    ///\n420\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n421\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n422\t    /// ```\n423\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n424\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n425\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n426\t        {\n427\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n428\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n429\t            log_debug!(\n430\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n431\t                scope_type,\n432\t                line,\n433\t                indent_level,\n434\t                parent_info,\n435\t                self.depth(),\n436\t                self.get_scope_path()\n437\t            );\n438\t        }\n439\t\n440\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n441\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n442\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n443\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n444\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n445\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n446\t\n447\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n448\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\n449\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n450\t\n451\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n452\t            {\n453\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n454\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n455\t                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n456\t                    .collect();\n457\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n458\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n459\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n460\t                             scopes_to_remove);\n461\t                }\n462\t            }\n463\t\n464\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n465\t\n466\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n467\t            {\n468\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n469\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n470\t                if removed_count > 0 {\n471\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n472\t                             removed_count,\n473\t                             before_path,\n474\t                             self.get_scope_path());\n475\t                }\n476\t            }\n477\t\n478\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n479\t\n480\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n481\t            {\n482\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n483\t            }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            // Create new scope\n486\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n487\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n488\t\n489\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n490\t            {\n491\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n492\t            }\n493\t        }\n494\t\n495\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n496\t        {\n497\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n498\t        }\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    /// Exit one level to immediate parent scope\n502\t    ///\n503\t    /// This method exits from the current scope to its immediate parent scope.\n504\t    /// It's a convenience method for single-level scope exits.\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// # Returns\n507\t    ///\n508\t    /// `true` if successfully exited to parent, `false` if already at root scope\n509\t    ///\n510\t    /// # Examples\n511\t    ///\n512\t    /// ```\n513\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n514\t    ///\n515\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n516\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n517\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n518\t    ///\n519\t    /// // Exit from child to parent\n520\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n521\t    /// assert!(exited);\n522\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n523\t    ///\n524\t    /// // Exit from parent to root\n525\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n526\t    /// assert!(exited);\n527\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// // Already at root - cannot exit further\n530\t    /// let exited = stack.exit_one_level();\n531\t    /// assert!(!exited);\n532\t    /// ```\n533\t    pub fn exit_one_level(&mut self) -> bool {\n534\t        // Edge case: already at root scope (depth of 1)\n535\t        if self.depth() <= 1 {\n536\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n537\t            {\n538\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT ONE LEVEL] Already at root scope (depth={}), cannot exit further\", self.depth());\n539\t            }\n540\t            return false;\n541\t        }\n542\t\n543\t        // Get the current scope's indent level\n544\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n545\t\n546\t        // Find the parent scope's indent level\n547\t        // The parent is the second-to-last scope in the stack\n548\t        let parent_indent = if self.depth() >= 2 {\n549\t            self.scopes[self.depth() - 2].indent_level\n550\t        } else {\n551\t            // Should not reach here due to the depth check above,\n552\t            // but handle gracefully by defaulting to root indent (0)\n553\t            0\n554\t        };\n555\t\n556\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n557\t        {\n558\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n559\t            log_debug!(\n560\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT ONE LEVEL] exiting from indent={} to parent indent={}, path='{}'\",\n561\t                current_indent,\n562\t                parent_indent,\n563\t                before_path\n564\t            );\n565\t        }\n566\t\n567\t        // Use the existing exit_to_scope method with the parent's indent\n568\t        self.exit_to_scope(parent_indent);\n569\t\n570\t        true\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n574\t    ///\n575\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n576\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n577\t    ///\n578\t    /// # Arguments\n579\t    ///\n580\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n581\t    ///\n582\t    /// # Examples\n583\t    ///\n584\t    /// ```\n585\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n586\t    ///\n587\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n588\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n589\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n590\t    /// ```\n591\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n592\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n593\t        {\n594\t            log_debug!(\n595\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n596\t                target_indent,\n597\t                self.depth(),\n598\t                self.current_indent(),\n599\t                self.get_scope_path()\n600\t            );\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n604\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n605\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n606\t            {\n607\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n608\t            }\n609\t            return;\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n613\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n614\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n615\t            .count() == 0;\n616\t\n617\t        if would_be_empty {\n618\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n619\t            {\n620\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n621\t            }\n622\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n623\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n624\t            return;\n625\t        }\n626\t\n627\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n628\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n629\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n630\t\n631\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n632\t        {\n633\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n634\t        }\n635\t\n636\t        // Search for target scope in hierarchy\n637\t        // This handles cases where the exact target indent doesn't exist in the stack\n638\t        // by finding the closest parent scope or creating a fallback if needed\n639\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n640\t            // No exact match at target indent - search for closest parent\n641\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n642\t            {\n643\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at target_indent={}, searching for closest parent scope\",\n644\t                         target_indent);\n645\t            }\n646\t\n647\t            // Find the closest scope with indent <= target_indent\n648\t            let closest_scope = self.scopes.iter()\n649\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n650\t                .max_by_key(|s| s.indent_level);\n651\t\n652\t            match closest_scope {\n653\t                Some(scope) => {\n654\t                    // Found a parent scope - use that instead\n655\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n656\t                    {\n657\t                        log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found closest parent scope at indent={}, path='{}', using existing scope\",\n658\t                                 scope.indent_level, self.get_scope_path());\n659\t                    }\n660\t                    // We've already retained scopes <= target_indent, so we're at the closest scope\n661\t                }\n662\t                None => {\n663\t                    // No suitable parent scope found - this shouldn't happen in normal YAML\n664\t                    // but we handle it gracefully by creating a fallback scope\n665\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n666\t                    {\n667\t                        log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No suitable parent scope found, creating fallback scope at indent={}\",\n668\t                                 target_indent);\n669\t                    }\n670\t                    let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n671\t                    self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n672\t                }\n673\t            }\n674\t        }\n675\t\n676\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n677\t        {\n678\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n679\t        }\n680\t    }\n681\t\n682\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n683\t    ///\n684\t    /// # Arguments\n685\t    ///\n686\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n687\t    ///\n688\t    /// # Returns\n689\t    ///\n690\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n691\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n692\t        self.scopes.last()\n693\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n694\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n698\t    ///\n699\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n700\t    ///\n701\t    /// # Arguments\n702\t    ///\n703\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n704\t    ///\n705\t    /// # Returns\n706\t    ///\n707\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n708\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n709\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n710\t    }\n711\t\n712\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n713\t    ///\n714\t    /// # Arguments\n715\t    ///\n716\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n717\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n718\t    ///\n719\t    /// # Returns\n720\t    ///\n721\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n722\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n723\t    ///\n724\t    /// # Examples\n725\t    ///\n726\t    /// ```\n727\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n728\t    ///\n729\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n730\t    ///\n731\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n732\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n733\t    ///\n734\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n735\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n736\t    /// ```\n737\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n738\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n739\t\n740\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n741\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n742\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n743\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n744\t                key: key.to_string(),\n745\t                scope_path,\n746\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n747\t                duplicate_line: line,\n748\t            })\n749\t        } else {\n750\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n751\t            scope.add_key(key);\n752\t            Ok(())\n753\t        }\n754\t    }\n755\t\n756\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n757\t    ///\n758\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n759\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n760\t    ///\n761\t    /// # Returns\n762\t    ///\n763\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n764\t    ///\n765\t    /// # Examples\n766\t    ///\n767\t    /// ```\n768\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n769\t    ///\n770\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n771\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n772\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n773\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n774\t    /// ```\n775\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n776\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n777\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n778\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n779\t                path.push(key.clone());\n780\t            }\n781\t        }\n782\t        path.join(\".\")\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Get current indent level\n786\t    ///\n787\t    /// # Returns\n788\t    ///\n789\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n790\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n791\t        self.scopes.last()\n792\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n793\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n794\t    }\n795\t\n796\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n797\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n798\t        self.scopes.len()\n799\t    }\n800\t\n801\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n802\t    ///\n803\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n804\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n805\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n806\t        self.clear_indent_transitions();\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n810\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n811\t        self.base_indent\n812\t    }\n813\t\n814\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n815\t    ///\n816\t    /// # Arguments\n817\t    ///\n818\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n819\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n820\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n821\t    }\n822\t\n823\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n824\t    ///\n825\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n826\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n827\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n828\t    ///\n829\t    /// # Arguments\n830\t    ///\n831\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n832\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n833\t    ///\n834\t    /// # Examples\n835\t    ///\n836\t    /// ```\n837\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n838\t    ///\n839\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n840\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n841\t    /// ```\n842\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n843\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n844\t        {\n845\t            log_debug!(\n846\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n847\t                line,\n848\t                indent_level,\n849\t                self.depth(),\n850\t                self.get_scope_path()\n851\t            );\n852\t        }\n853\t\n854\t        // Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\n855\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we clear any existing scope at this level\n856\t        // (including parent mappings) and all deeper scopes, then add a new sequence scope\n857\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n858\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n859\t\n860\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n861\t        {\n862\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n863\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level)\n864\t                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n865\t                .collect();\n866\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n867\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} scopes at or deeper than indent={}: {:?}\",\n868\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n869\t                         indent_level,\n870\t                         scopes_to_remove);\n871\t            }\n872\t        }\n873\t\n874\t        // Only retain scopes shallower than indent_level\n875\t        // Sequence scopes completely replace any mapping at the same level\n876\t        // (unlike sibling mappings which preserve the parent)\n877\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n878\t\n879\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n880\t        {\n881\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n882\t            if removed_count > 0 {\n883\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n884\t                         removed_count,\n885\t                         before_path,\n886\t                         self.get_scope_path());\n887\t            }\n888\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes at or deeper than this level (replaces any parent mapping)\", indent_level);\n889\t        }\n890\t\n891\t        // Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n892\t        // Each sequence item needs a fresh scope - we don't reuse existing sequence scopes\n893\t        let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n894\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n895\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n896\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n897\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n898\t\n899\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n900\t        {\n901\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n902\t        }\n903\t\n904\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n905\t        {\n906\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n907\t        }\n908\t    }\n909\t\n910\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n911\t    ///\n912\t    /// # Returns\n913\t    ///\n914\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n915\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n916\t        self.scopes.last()\n917\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n918\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n919\t    }\n920\t\n921\t    /// Record an indent transition\n922\t    ///\n923\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n924\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n925\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n926\t    ///\n927\t    /// # Arguments\n928\t    ///\n929\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n930\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n931\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n932\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n933\t    ///\n934\t    /// Record an indent transition\n935\t    ///\n936\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n937\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n938\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n939\t    ///\n940\t    /// # Arguments\n941\t    ///\n942\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n943\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n944\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n945\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n946\t    ///\n947\t    /// # Examples\n948\t    ///\n949\t    /// ```\n950\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n951\t    ///\n952\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n953\t    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \"  # blank line with indent\");\n954\t    /// ```\n955\t    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n956\t        // Only record if the indent actually changed\n957\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n958\t            // Store the old indent before updating\n959\t            let old_indent = self.last_indent;\n960\t\n961\t            // Classify the line type\n962\t            let line_classification = classify_line_type(raw_line);\n963\t\n964\t            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n965\t                line_number,\n966\t                old_indent,\n967\t                new_indent,\n968\t                has_key,\n969\t                raw_line,\n970\t                line_classification,\n971\t            );\n972\t            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n973\t            self.last_indent = new_indent;\n974\t\n975\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n976\t            {\n977\t                let direction = if new_indent > old_indent {\n978\t                    \"increase\"\n979\t                } else {\n980\t                    \"decrease\"\n981\t                };\n982\t                let key_status = if has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n983\t                log_debug!(\n984\t                    \"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, {}, raw='{}'\",\n985\t                    line_number,\n986\t                    old_indent,\n987\t                    new_indent,\n988\t                    key_status,\n989\t                    line_classification,\n990\t                    raw_line.trim()\n991\t                );\n992\t            }\n993\t        }\n994\t    }\n995\t\n996\t    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\n997\t    ///\n998\t    /// # Returns\n999\t    ///\n1000\t    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\n1001\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\n1002\t        &self.indent_transitions\n1003\t    }\n1004\t\n1005\t    /// Get indent transitions without keys\n1006\t    ///\n1007\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\n1008\t    /// such as blank lines or comments.\n1009\t    ///\n1010\t    /// # Returns\n1011\t    ///\n1012\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\n1013\t    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1014\t        self.indent_transitions\n1015\t            .iter()\n1016\t            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\n1017\t            .collect()\n1018\t    }\n1019\t\n1020\t    /// Get indent transitions with keys\n1021\t    ///\n1022\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\n1023\t    ///\n1024\t    /// # Returns\n1025\t    ///\n1026\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\n1027\t    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1028\t        self.indent_transitions\n1029\t            .iter()\n1030\t            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\n1031\t            .collect()\n1032\t    }\n1033\t\n1034\t    /// Get enter-scope transitions\n1035\t    ///\n1036\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent entering a deeper scope.\n1037\t    ///\n1038\t    /// # Returns\n1039\t    ///\n1040\t    /// A vector of enter-scope transitions\n1041\t    pub fn get_enter_scope_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1042\t        self.indent_transitions\n1043\t            .iter()\n1044\t            .filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope())\n1045\t            .collect()\n1046\t    }\n1047\t\n1048\t    /// Get exit-scope transitions\n1049\t    ///\n1050\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent exiting to a parent scope.\n1051\t    ///\n1052\t    /// # Returns\n1053\t    ///\n1054\t    /// A vector of exit-scope transitions\n1055\t    pub fn get_exit_scope_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1056\t        self.indent_transitions\n1057\t            .iter()\n1058\t            .filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope())\n1059\t            .collect()\n1060\t    }\n1061\t\n1062\t    /// Get same-level transitions\n1063\t    ///\n1064\t    /// Returns only those transitions that represent staying at the same level.\n1065\t    ///\n1066\t    /// # Returns\n1067\t    ///\n1068\t    /// A vector of same-level transitions\n1069\t    pub fn get_same_level_transitions(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n1070\t        self.indent_transitions\n1071\t            .iter()\n1072\t            .filter(|t| t.is_same_level())\n1073\t            .collect()\n1074\t    }\n1075\t\n1076\t    /// Get transition count by type\n1077\t    ///\n1078\t    /// Returns a tuple of (enter_count, exit_count, same_level_count).\n1079\t    ///\n1080\t    /// # Returns\n1081\t    ///\n1082\t    /// A tuple with counts of each transition type\n1083\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n1084\t        let enter = self.get_enter_scope_transitions().len();\n1085\t        let exit = self.get_exit_scope_transitions().len();\n1086\t        let same = self.get_same_level_transitions().len();\n1087\t        (enter, exit, same)\n1088\t    }\n1089\t\n1090\t    /// Clear all indent transitions\n1091\t    ///\n1092\t    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\n1093\t    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\n1094\t        self.indent_transitions.clear();\n1095\t        self.last_indent = 0;\n1096\t    }\n1097\t\n1098\t    /// Get the last recorded indent level\n1099\t    ///\n1100\t    /// # Returns\n1101\t    ///\n1102\t    /// The most recent indent level recorded\n1103\t    pub fn get_last_indent(&self) -> usize {\n1104\t        self.last_indent\n1105\t    }\n1106\t\n1107\t    /// Set the last indent level directly\n1108\t    ///\n1109\t    /// This can be used to initialize the indent tracking or to correct it after\n1110\t    /// document markers or other special constructs.\n1111\t    ///\n1112\t    /// # Arguments\n1113\t    ///\n1114\t    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\n1115\t    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\n1116\t        self.last_indent = indent;\n1117\t    }\n1118\t\n1119\t    /// Process an indent transition without a key token\n1120\t    ///\n1121\t    /// This method handles indentation changes that occur on lines without key tokens,\n1122\t    /// such as blank lines or comments. It determines if the indent change represents\n1123\t    /// a valid scope transition and triggers the appropriate scope entry/exit.\n1124\t    ///\n1125\t    /// # Arguments\n1126\t    ///\n1127\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1128\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n1129\t    ///\n1130\t    /// # Returns\n1131\t    ///\n1132\t    /// `true` if a scope transition occurred, `false` otherwise\n1133\t    ///\n1134\t    /// # Examples\n1135\t    ///\n1136\t    /// ```\n1137\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n1138\t    ///\n1139\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n1140\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n1141\t    /// // Process indent decrease on blank line\n1142\t    /// let transitioned = stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(5, 0);\n1143\t    /// assert!(transitioned); // Should exit to root scope\n1144\t    /// ```\n1145\t    pub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\n1146\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n1147\t\n1148\t        // Compare new indent with current scope indent\n1149\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n1150\t\n1151\t        match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\n1152\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n1153\t                // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\n1154\t                // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\n1155\t                // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\n1156\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n1157\t                {\n1158\t                    log_debug!(\n1159\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (increase), NO SCOPE ENTRY (no parent key)\",\n1160\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent\n1161\t                    );\n1162\t                }\n1163\t                false\n1164\t            }\n1165\t            Ordering::Less => {\n1166\t                // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n1167\t                // This is valid even without a key (e.g., blank line at end of nested block)\n1168\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n1169\t                {\n1170\t                    let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n1171\t                    log_debug!(\n1172\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (decrease), exiting scope: '{}'\",\n1173\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent, before_path\n1174\t                    );\n1175\t                }\n1176\t\n1177\t                self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\n1178\t                true\n1179\t            }\n1180\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n1181\t                // Same indent - no scope change needed\n1182\t                false\n1183\t            }\n1184\t        }\n1185\t    }\n1186\t}\n1187\t\n1188\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n1189\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1190\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n1191\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n1192\t    }\n1193\t}\n1194\t\n1195\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n1196\t///\n1197\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n1198\t/// in the current scope.\n1199\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n1200\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n1201\t    /// The duplicate key\n1202\t    pub key: String,\n1203\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n1204\t    pub scope_path: String,\n1205\t    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n1206\t    pub first_line: usize,\n1207\t    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n1208\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n1209\t}\n1210\t\n1211\timpl DuplicateKeyError {\n1212\t    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n1213\t    ///\n1214\t    /// # Arguments\n1215\t    ///\n1216\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n1217\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n1218\t    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n1219\t    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n1220\t    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n1221\t        Self {\n1222\t            key,\n1223\t            scope_path,\n1224\t            first_line,\n1225\t            duplicate_line,\n1226\t        }\n1227\t    }\n1228\t\n1229\t    /// Get a formatted error message\n1230\t    ///\n1231\t    /// # Returns\n1232\t    ///\n1233\t    /// A human-readable error message\n1234\t    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n1235\t        format!(\n1236\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n1237\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n1238\t        )\n1239\t    }\n1240\t}\n1241\t\n1242\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n1243\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1244\t        write!(\n1245\t            f,\n1246\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n1247\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n1248\t        )\n1249\t    }\n1250\t}\n1251\t\n1252\timpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n1253\t\n1254\t/// Key context classification\n1255\t///\n1256\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n1257\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n1258\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n1259\tpub enum KeyContext {\n1260\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n1261\t    ///\n1262\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n1263\t    InlineScalar {\n1264\t        /// The key name\n1265\t        key: String,\n1266\t        /// The scalar value\n1267\t        value: String,\n1268\t    },\n1269\t\n1270\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n1271\t    ///\n1272\t    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n1273\t    ParentMapping {\n1274\t        /// The parent key name\n1275\t        key: String,\n1276\t    },\n1277\t\n1278\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n1279\t    ///\n1280\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n1281\t    ParentSequence {\n1282\t        /// The parent key name\n1283\t        key: String,\n1284\t    },\n1285\t}\n1286\t\n1287\timpl KeyContext {\n1288\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n1289\t    ///\n1290\t    /// # Returns\n1291\t    ///\n1292\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n1293\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n1294\t        match self {\n1295\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n1296\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n1297\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n1298\t        }\n1299\t    }\n1300\t\n1301\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n1302\t    ///\n1303\t    /// # Returns\n1304\t    ///\n1305\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n1306\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n1307\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n1308\t    }\n1309\t\n1310\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n1311\t    ///\n1312\t    /// # Returns\n1313\t    ///\n1314\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n1315\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n1316\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n1317\t    }\n1318\t}\n1319\t\n1320\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n1321\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1322\t        match self {\n1323\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n1324\t                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n1325\t            }\n1326\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n1327\t                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n1328\t            }\n1329\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n1330\t                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n1331\t            }\n1332\t        }\n1333\t    }\n1334\t}\n1335\t\n1336\t/// Extract key context from a line\n1337\t///\n1338\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n1339\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n1340\t///\n1341\t/// # Arguments\n1342\t///\n1343\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n1344\t///\n1345\t/// # Returns\n1346\t///\n1347\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n1348\t///\n1349\t/// # Examples\n1350\t///\n1351\t/// ```\n1352\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n1353\t///\n1354\t/// // Inline scalar\n1355\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n1356\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n1357\t///\n1358\t/// // Parent mapping\n1359\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n1360\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n1361\t/// ```\n1362\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n1363\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n1364\t\n1365\t    // Find colon position\n1366\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n1367\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n1368\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n1369\t\n1370\t    // Skip if key is empty\n1371\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n1372\t    if key.is_empty() {\n1373\t        return None;\n1374\t    }\n1375\t\n1376\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n1377\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n1378\t        return None;\n1379\t    }\n1380\t\n1381\t    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\n1382\t    // Handles: \"- name: value\" -> key should be \"name\", not \"- name\"\n1383\t    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\"- \") {\n1384\t        // Remove the \"- \" prefix (dash and space)\n1385\t        key[2..].trim()\n1386\t    } else if key.starts_with('-') && key.len() > 1 {\n1387\t        // Dash followed by non-space (invalid, but handle gracefully)\n1388\t        &key[1..]\n1389\t    } else {\n1390\t        key\n1391\t    };\n1392\t\n1393\t    // Skip if actual key is empty after stripping dash\n1394\t    if actual_key.is_empty() {\n1395\t        return None;\n1396\t    }\n1397\t\n1398\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n1399\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n1400\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\n1401\t    } else {\n1402\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n1403\t            key: actual_key.to_string(),\n1404\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n1405\t        }\n1406\t    };\n1407\t\n1408\t    Some(context)\n1409\t}\n1410\t\n1411\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n1412\t///\n1413\t/// # Arguments\n1414\t///\n1415\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n1416\t///\n1417\t/// # Returns\n1418\t///\n1419\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n1420\t///\n1421\t/// # Examples\n1422\t///\n1423\t/// ```\n1424\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n1425\t///\n1426\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n1427\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n1428\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n1429\t/// ```\n1430\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n1431\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n1432\t}\n1433\t\n1434\t/// Classify a YAML line as key-bearing or indent-only\n1435\t///\n1436\t/// This function analyzes a line to determine if it contains a key token,\n1437\t/// which is essential for proper scope transition handling.\n1438\t///\n1439\t/// # Arguments\n1440\t///\n1441\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to classify\n1442\t///\n1443\t/// # Returns\n1444\t///\n1445\t/// The `LineClassification` for the line\n1446\t///\n1447\t/// # Examples\n1448\t///\n1449\t/// ```\n1450\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::classify_line_type;\n1451\t///\n1452\t/// // Key-bearing lines\n1453\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"key: value\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1454\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"  nested:\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1455\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"- item\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::KeyBearing));\n1456\t///\n1457\t/// // Indent-only lines (no key token)\n1458\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"  some value\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::IndentOnly));\n1459\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"    more text\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::IndentOnly));\n1460\t///\n1461\t/// // Empty lines\n1462\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"\"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::Empty));\n1463\t/// assert!(matches!(classify_line_type(\"    \"), armor::parsers::yaml::scope::LineClassification::Empty));\n1464\t/// ```\n1465\tpub fn classify_line_type(line: &str) -> LineClassification {\n1466\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n1467\t\n1468\t    // Empty or whitespace-only lines\n1469\t    if trimmed.is_empty() {\n1470\t        return LineClassification::Empty;\n1471\t    }\n1472\t\n1473\t    // Check if line has a key token by using existing extract_key_context\n1474\t    // This handles all YAML key patterns including:\n1475\t    // - Simple keys: \"key: value\"\n1476\t    // - Parent keys: \"key:\"\n1477\t    // - Sequence items with keys: \"- key: value\"\n1478\t    // - Nested keys with proper indentation\n1479\t    if extract_key_context(line).is_some() {\n1480\t        LineClassification::KeyBearing\n1481\t    } else {\n1482\t        LineClassification::IndentOnly\n1483\t    }\n1484\t}\n1485\t\n1486\t/// Check if a line contains key tokens\n1487\t///\n1488\t/// This is a convenience function that returns true if the line is key-bearing.\n1489\t///\n1490\t/// # Arguments\n1491\t///\n1492\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to check\n1493\t///\n1494\t/// # Returns\n1495\t///\n1496\t/// `true` if the line contains key tokens, `false` otherwise\n1497\t///\n1498\t/// # Examples\n1499\t///\n1500\t/// ```\n1501\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::has_key_token;\n1502\t///\n1503\t/// assert!(has_key_token(\"key: value\"));\n1504\t/// assert!(has_key_token(\"  nested: value\"));\n1505\t/// assert!(!has_key_token(\"  some text\"));\n1506\t/// assert!(!has_key_token(\"\"));\n1507\t/// ```\n1508\tpub fn has_key_token(line: &str) -> bool {\n1509\t    classify_line_type(line).is_key_bearing()\n1510\t}\n1511\t\n1512\t/// Line classification for YAML parsing\n1513\t///\n1514\t/// This enum categorizes lines based on whether they contain key tokens,\n1515\t/// which is essential for proper scope transition handling.\n1516\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n1517\tpub enum LineClassification {\n1518\t    /// Key-bearing line (contains a YAML key token like \"key:\", \"- key:\", etc.)\n1519\t    KeyBearing,\n1520\t\n1521\t    /// Indent-only line (no key token, only whitespace/content)\n1522\t    IndentOnly,\n1523\t\n1524\t    /// Empty or whitespace-only line\n1525\t    Empty,\n1526\t}\n1527\t\n1528\timpl LineClassification {\n1529\t    /// Check if this line type is key-bearing\n1530\t    pub fn is_key_bearing(&self) -> bool {\n1531\t        matches!(self, Self::KeyBearing)\n1532\t    }\n1533\t\n1534\t    /// Check if this line type is indent-only\n1535\t    pub fn is_indent_only(&self) -> bool {\n1536\t        matches!(self, Self::IndentOnly)\n1537\t    }\n1538\t\n1539\t    /// Check if this line type is empty\n1540\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n1541\t        matches!(self, Self::Empty)\n1542\t    }\n1543\t}\n1544\t\n1545\timpl fmt::Display for LineClassification {\n1546\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1547\t        match self {\n1548\t            Self::KeyBearing => write!(f, \"key-bearing\"),\n1549\t            Self::IndentOnly => write!(f, \"indent-only\"),\n1550\t            Self::Empty => write!(f, \"empty\"),\n1551\t        }\n1552\t    }\n1553\t}\n1554\t\n1555\t/// Indent transition event recorded during parsing\n1556\t///\n1557\t/// This struct captures information about indentation level changes that occur\n1558\t/// during YAML parsing, whether or not a key token is present.\n1559\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n1560\tpub struct IndentTransition {\n1561\t    /// The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1562\t    pub line_number: usize,\n1563\t    /// The previous indentation level\n1564\t    pub from_indent: usize,\n1565\t    /// The new indentation level\n1566\t    pub to_indent: usize,\n1567\t    /// Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1568\t    pub has_key: bool,\n1569\t    /// The raw line content (for debugging)\n1570\t    pub raw_line: String,\n1571\t    /// Line classification (key-bearing vs indent-only)\n1572\t    pub line_classification: LineClassification,\n1573\t    /// Classification of the transition type (enter-scope, exit-scope, same-level)\n1574\t    pub transition_type: IndentTransitionType,\n1575\t}\n1576\t\n1577\timpl IndentTransition {\n1578\t    /// Create a new indent transition record\n1579\t    ///\n1580\t    /// # Arguments\n1581\t    ///\n1582\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1583\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n1584\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n1585\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1586\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\n1587\t    /// * `line_classification` - Classification of the line type\n1588\t    pub fn new(line_number: usize, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str, line_classification: LineClassification) -> Self {\n1589\t        let transition_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(from_indent, to_indent);\n1590\t        Self {\n1591\t            line_number,\n1592\t            from_indent,\n1593\t            to_indent,\n1594\t            has_key,\n1595\t            raw_line: raw_line.to_string(),\n1596\t            line_classification,\n1597\t            transition_type,\n1598\t        }\n1599\t    }\n1600\t\n1601\t    /// Check if this is an indent increase\n1602\t    pub fn is_increase(&self) -> bool {\n1603\t        self.to_indent > self.from_indent\n1604\t    }\n1605\t\n1606\t    /// Check if this is an indent decrease\n1607\t    pub fn is_decrease(&self) -> bool {\n1608\t        self.to_indent < self.from_indent\n1609\t    }\n1610\t\n1611\t    /// Get the indent change amount (positive for increase, negative for decrease)\n1612\t    pub fn change_amount(&self) -> isize {\n1613\t        self.to_indent as isize - self.from_indent as isize\n1614\t    }\n1615\t\n1616\t    /// Check if this transition occurred without a key\n1617\t    pub fn is_without_key(&self) -> bool {\n1618\t        !self.has_key\n1619\t    }\n1620\t\n1621\t    /// Get the line classification for this transition\n1622\t    pub fn line_classification(&self) -> LineClassification {\n1623\t        self.line_classification\n1624\t    }\n1625\t\n1626\t    /// Get the transition type (enter-scope, exit-scope, same-level)\n1627\t    pub fn transition_type(&self) -> IndentTransitionType {\n1628\t        self.transition_type\n1629\t    }\n1630\t\n1631\t    /// Check if this is an enter-scope transition\n1632\t    pub fn is_enter_scope(&self) -> bool {\n1633\t        self.transition_type.is_enter_scope()\n1634\t    }\n1635\t\n1636\t    /// Check if this is an exit-scope transition\n1637\t    pub fn is_exit_scope(&self) -> bool {\n1638\t        self.transition_type.is_exit_scope()\n1639\t    }\n1640\t\n1641\t    /// Check if this is a same-level transition\n1642\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n1643\t        self.transition_type.is_same_level()\n1644\t    }\n1645\t\n1646\t    /// Map this indent transition to a scope operation description\n1647\t    ///\n1648\t    /// Returns a human-readable description of what scope operation\n1649\t    /// this transition represents.\n1650\t    pub fn scope_operation(&self) -> &'static str {\n1651\t        match self.transition_type {\n1652\t            IndentTransitionType::EnterScope => \"enter-scope\",\n1653\t            IndentTransitionType::ExitScope => \"exit-scope\",\n1654\t            IndentTransitionType::SameLevel => \"stay-in-scope\",\n1655\t        }\n1656\t    }\n1657\t}\n1658\t\n1659\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransition {\n1660\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1661\t        let direction = if self.is_increase() {\n1662\t            \"increase\"\n1663\t        } else if self.is_decrease() {\n1664\t            \"decrease\"\n1665\t        } else {\n1666\t            \"no-change\"\n1667\t        };\n1668\t        let key_status = if self.has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n1669\t        write!(\n1670\t            f,\n1671\t            \"IndentTransition(line={}, {}→{}, {}, {}, {}, type={})\",\n1672\t            self.line_number, self.from_indent, self.to_indent, direction, key_status, self.line_classification, self.transition_type\n1673\t        )\n1674\t    }\n1675\t}\n1676\t\n1677\t// Tests for indent transition tracking\n1678\t#[cfg(test)]\n1679\tmod tests {\n1680\t    use super::*;\n1681\t\n1682\t    #[test]\n1683\t    fn test_indent_transition_type_classify() {\n1684\t        // Test enter-scope (indent increase)\n1685\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(0, 2);\n1686\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope);\n1687\t        assert!(trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1688\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1689\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_same_level());\n1690\t\n1691\t        // Test exit-scope (indent decrease)\n1692\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(4, 2);\n1693\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope);\n1694\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1695\t        assert!(trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1696\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_same_level());\n1697\t\n1698\t        // Test same-level (no indent change)\n1699\t        let trans_type = IndentTransitionType::classify(2, 2);\n1700\t        assert_eq!(trans_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel);\n1701\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_enter_scope());\n1702\t        assert!(!trans_type.is_exit_scope());\n1703\t        assert!(trans_type.is_same_level());\n1704\t    }\n1705\t\n1706\t    #[test]\n1707\t    fn test_indent_transition_type_display() {\n1708\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", \");\n1864\t\n1865\t        let transitions = stack.get_indent_transitions();\n1866\t        assert_eq!(transitions.len(), 3);\n1867\t\n1868\t        // Check first transition (enter-scope)\n1869\t
